message:
test(fuzz): add x25519 DH symmetry + zero-share rejection target (resolves hlnm4)

Adds fuzz_x25519_dh covering fcp_crypto::x25519, the Diffie-Hellman
primitive that underpins mesh-session key derivation, HPKE
encapsulation, and any wire-supplied X25519PublicKey.

Properties asserted:

  1. X25519SecretKey/X25519PublicKey from_bytes are total over
     arbitrary 32-byte inputs.
  2. X25519PublicKey::try_from_slice length gate rejects every slice
     whose length is not exactly 32 bytes.
  3. DH symmetry: sk_a·pk_b == sk_b·pk_a for any non-degenerate (a, b).
     The match arm panics on asymmetric outcomes — if one direction
     succeeds and the other rejects, the implementation has asymmetric
     handling of low-order inputs (subgroup-defense bypass surface).
  4. Pubkey serialization round-trip: pk → to_bytes → from_bytes
     preserves both byte representation and key_id.
  5. Once-gated low-order anchor: the all-zero peer pubkey MUST trip
     InvalidPublicKey (the guard at x25519.rs:79-91 closes the
     small-subgroup confinement attack documented in RFC 7748 §6.1).
     Anchored against a non-degenerate DH success to ensure the guard
     is not over-restrictive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hk0uj","title":"[SEC-AUDIT] fcp-host missing zone policy falls back to allow-all runtime policy","description":"crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:892-904 returns host_runtime_policy() when a zone has no configured policy, and crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:1607-1633 feeds that permissive object straight into simulate_policy_decision() during live preflight. Attack scenario: an operator intends per-zone connector/capability deny rules but omits one zone from FCP_HOST_ZONE_POLICIES_FILE (or loses it during rollout); any caller holding an otherwise-valid capability token for that zone bypasses all rule-based connector/capability transport denials because the fallback policy has empty deny/ceiling lists and allow_lan/allow_derp/allow_funnel=true. Proposed mitigation: fail closed when a live request targets a zone with no configured policy, or require an explicit per-zone opt-in marker before using the permissive host_runtime_policy fallback.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:16:54.311369Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:19:04.724768Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:19:04.724375Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hkhy","title":"[IDEA-17] Schema Hash + Canonical CBOR Vector Generator","description":"## Background
Spec and docs require deterministic CBOR + schema hash vectors, but maintaining them manually is error-prone. We need a generator to keep vectors and schema hashes in sync with the authoritative definitions.

## Goals
- Generate schema hashes and canonical CBOR vectors from source schemas.
- Emit stable JSON/CBOR fixtures for conformance tests.
- Integrate with CI to detect drift.

## Non-goals
- Auto-modify spec text or accept non-canonical encodings.

## Approach
- Add a generator tool in `fcp-conformance` (CLI subcommand).
- Parse schema definitions (CDDL/JSON schema or Rust type registry).
- Compute schema hash via existing canonicalization routines.
- Write vector files with deterministic ordering.

## Risks
- Schema evolution could break determinism; add fixtures and strict ordering rules.

## Success Criteria
- Generator produces identical outputs across runs/platforms.
- CI can regenerate and diff vectors without noise.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Regenerated vectors are byte‑for‑byte stable across two consecutive runs.
- Schema hash changes when a schema field definition changes.
- CI diff check fails on any vector drift.
","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

Epic complete. Vector generator CLI works, determinism tests pass (16/16), CI vector regen+diff is implemented.","created_at":"2026-03-01T21:07:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hlk2h","title":"[FCP3/P1.1a] Inventory execution, lifecycle, and runtime ownership leakage","description":"Background: the original P1.1 bead was too large and too serial. Execution and runtime ownership leakage alone spans core, host, SDK, CLI, supervisor, and connector lifecycle surfaces, so collapsing it into one front door bead made the entire initiative less parallel than it needs to be.

Scope: inventory where invoke, stream, drain, checkpoint, resume, health, budgeting, supervision, and execution form neutrality are actually owned today. Call out which semantics live in fcp-core, which are re owned by fcp-host or fcp-sdk, which reach through into fwc, and which facts exist only as host local runtime state.

Acceptance: there is a self contained execution and lifecycle leakage inventory that later synthesis work can merge into the unified migration baseline.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:22:37.168398Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:48.383129Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:48.382888Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["execution","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","inventory","mesh-first","phase-1","runtime"],"comments":[{"id":2059,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hlk2h","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass 2026-03-27: Covered by docs/FCP3_Semantic_Ownership_Inventory.md sections 2-4. Execution leakage inventory: fcp-core owns 24 execution types + FcpConnector trait. fcp-host owns ProcessState machine, supervisor, resilience (CircuitBreaker/Bulkhead/LoadShedder), cancellation, progress. Key reach-throughs: rollout.rs↔LifecycleManager, enforcement.rs↔PolicyEngine. fwc reach-through: readiness.rs imports fcp-core introspection types.","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:47Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-hlnm4","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X25519-DH: diffie_hellman symmetry + zero-share rejection","description":"Add fuzz target for fcp-crypto::x25519. The DH primitive is the basis for HPKE, mesh-session key derivation, and X25519PublicKey serde-decoded over the wire. Properties: (1) X25519SecretKey/X25519PublicKey from_bytes are total; (2) try_from_slice rejects wrong-length input; (3) diffie_hellman rejects all-zero shared secret (low-order/small-subgroup attack — see x25519.rs:79-91); (4) symmetry: sk_a.diffie_hellman(pk_b) == sk_b.diffie_hellman(pk_a) for any non-degenerate (a, b); (5) pubkey serialization round-trip via from_bytes(pk.to_bytes()). Existing fuzz_crypto_hpke_open / symbol_envelope_decrypt cover HPKE wrappers but not the DH primitive directly.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:43:11.426749Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:43:16.126482Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","tests"]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-hlnm4","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X25519-DH: diffie_hellman symmetry + zero-share rejection","description":"Add fuzz target for fcp-crypto::x25519. The DH primitive is the basis for HPKE, mesh-session key derivation, and X25519PublicKey serde-decoded over the wire. Properties: (1) X25519SecretKey/X25519PublicKey from_bytes are total; (2) try_from_slice rejects wrong-length input; (3) diffie_hellman rejects all-zero shared secret (low-order/small-subgroup attack — see x25519.rs:79-91); (4) symmetry: sk_a.diffie_hellman(pk_b) == sk_b.diffie_hellman(pk_a) for any non-degenerate (a, b); (5) pubkey serialization round-trip via from_bytes(pk.to_bytes()). Existing fuzz_crypto_hpke_open / symbol_envelope_decrypt cover HPKE wrappers but not the DH primitive directly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:43:11.426749Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:46:24.938802Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:46:24.938301Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3007,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hlnm4","author":"jemanuel","text":"Added fuzz_x25519_dh. Properties: (1) from_bytes total on both keys; (2) try_from_slice length gate (rejects all len ≠ 32); (3) DH symmetry — sk_a·pk_b == sk_b·pk_a, with asymmetric-outcome panic catching subgroup-defense bypass; (4) pubkey serialization round-trip (to_bytes→from_bytes preserves bytes + key_id); (5) Once-gated low-order anchor — all-zero peer pubkey MUST trip InvalidPublicKey, plus a non-degenerate DH success anchor to ensure the guard isn't over-restrictive. Compile + clippy + rustfmt clean via rch with local fallback.","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:46:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu","title":"[FCP3/P6.1] Replace fwc reach through with stable host and mesh backed contracts","description":"Background: fwc currently reaches into broad semantic surfaces to classify truth and readiness. That made sense while the host first world was the only truthful one, but it preserves architectural blur once lower layers become first class owners.

Scope: replace direct CLI reach through into broad semantic buckets with stable contracts exposed by the host and mesh backed architecture. Preserve machine readable user facing surfaces where they are intentionally supported, add explicit provenance and evidence handle fields so humans and scripts can see whether a claim is offline, node local, mesh backed, degraded, or fallback derived, and keep operator workflows truthful without forcing them to scrape prose. The work should also define contract fixtures, output snapshot tests, and detailed structured diagnostic fields so later acceptance scripts can detect regressions in both semantics and usability.

Acceptance: fwc depends on explicit operator facing contracts rather than on internal semantic grab bags, and the resulting CLI surfaces are easier for both humans and automation to trust, test, and consume.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TopazWren","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:07.971349Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:18:17.730859Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:18:17.730607Z","close_reason":"Completed the operator-truth contract track: hoogu.1 froze the stable schema, hoogu.2 moved remaining show/status/health/mesh/install CLI surfaces onto the typed contract builders, and hoogu.3 added reusable fixture-backed proof coverage for structured payloads and human summaries. Verification included rch-based cargo checks for the adoption layer and rch cargo test --test cual_integration operator_truth_fixture -- --nocapture for the proof layer.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fwc","mesh-first","phase-6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:46.442370Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1fu5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:45.745529Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:45.077502Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2235,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu","author":"WildTern","text":"[2026-04-01] Claimed a second narrow hoogu slice in crates/fwc/src/main.rs. Live mesh payloads now emit mesh-live-availability plus host-artifact-provenance / mesh-placement-policy / host-runtime-drift handles when present; offline mesh payloads now emit workspace-manifest and optional local-mesh-mirror handles. I fixed that by making cancel output outcome-aware for Cancelled, Pending, TooLate, and Failed, so the machine-readable payload now explains whether the cancellation actually happened, is still running, arrived too late, or failed, and it suggests relevant next actions (watch vs history/status) instead of a hard-coded error-history lookup. I also added explicit TooLate test coverage alongside the existing success/failure cancel contract assertions. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 and git diff --check passed.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.1","title":"[FCP3/P6.1] Freeze the stable CLI contract schema for operator truth surfaces","description":"Background: the remaining CLI reach-through problem starts with contract shape. Until the project freezes one explicit operator-facing contract for readiness, show, health, and provenance-bearing status, later work will keep binding `fwc` to internal crate layout instead of to durable interfaces.

Scope: define the stable host or mesh-backed contract schema that `fwc` should consume for operator-critical truth surfaces. Cover machine-readable field names, provenance taxonomy, evidence-handle references, degraded/fallback markers, and the subset of human-facing summaries that must remain stable enough for operator tooling and docs.

Acceptance: the repo has a concrete contract fixture set for operator truth surfaces, with explicit fields for offline, node-local, mesh-backed, degraded, and fallback-derived answers. Future CLI work should be able to depend on these fixtures instead of re-reading internal implementation code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:52.216770Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-03T21:01:41.870357Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T21:01:41.870180Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fwc","phase-6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:52.216770Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2410,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.1","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group contract note: these beads together define, adopt, and prove the new operator contract. Keep them tied to fixture-backed schemas, nearby unit or snapshot coverage, host-backed integration checks where applicable, redaction-safe structured logs, and explicit `rch` rerun commands so downstream live-truth, operator-evidence, and docs work can reuse the contract instead of re-deriving it.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:10Z"},{"id":2446,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Focused  verification surfaced one remaining contract mismatch: offline \ already exposes manifest lifecycle status () while keeping runtime . I updated the frozen operator-truth fixture/schema, the integration helper, the offline acceptance test, and the playbook so the contract now states that distinction explicitly.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:54:34Z"},{"id":2447,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Focused rch verification surfaced one remaining contract mismatch: offline show already exposes manifest lifecycle status (connector.status=ready) while keeping runtime connector.state=unknown. I updated the frozen operator-truth fixture/schema, the integration helper, the offline acceptance test, and the playbook so the contract now states that distinction explicitly.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:55:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.2","title":"[FCP3/P6.1] Move host and CLI implementations onto the frozen operator contracts","description":"Background: contract fixtures only matter if the host and CLI actually adopt them. Otherwise the repo still carries a truthful schema alongside a separate reach-through implementation.

Scope: implement the provider side and consumer side of the stable CLI contracts. Replace the remaining internal reach-through call sites with explicit contract fetches, ensure the host or mesh layer populates provenance and evidence fields from the real owners, and remove the most direct semantic-bucket dependencies from `fwc`.

Acceptance: `fwc` resolves operator-critical truth through explicit contracts rather than by reaching into semantic buckets, and regression fixtures make it obvious when the contract/provider/consumer relationship drifts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:52.800516Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:01:54.668946Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:01:54.668359Z","close_reason":"Verified adoption layer with rch cargo check --all-targets and rch cargo test --test cual_integration operator_truth_fixture -- --nocapture; proof-layer follow-up remains in hoogu.3.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fwc","phase-6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:52.800516Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:56:40.798497Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2411,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.2","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group contract note: these beads together define, adopt, and prove the new operator contract. Keep them tied to fixture-backed schemas, nearby unit or snapshot coverage, host-backed integration checks where applicable, redaction-safe structured logs, and explicit `rch` rerun commands so downstream live-truth, operator-evidence, and docs work can reuse the contract instead of re-deriving it.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:11Z"}]}
